questions.question: Which EC2 instance family is designed for GPU-accelerated ML training?
questions.explanation: P-series instances have NVIDIA data-centre GPUs. P3=V100, P4d=A100. G-series are for graphics/inference.

questions.question: What is a Spot Instance and why use it for ML training?
options: An interruptible instance up to 90% cheaper than On-Demand
questions.explanation: Spot uses spare AWS capacity at deep discounts. For training: save checkpoints frequently so a 2-minute interruption notice doesn't lose all progress.

questions.question: Which AWS service provides managed ML training without managing EC2 directly?
questions.explanation: SageMaker handles instance provisioning, training, and auto-termination. EC2 gives more control but requires manual setup.

questions.question: What is the purpose of an IAM role for an EC2 GPU instance running ML?
options: Grants the instance permission to access S3, ECR, and other AWS services without hardcoding credentials
questions.explanation: IAM roles = temporary credentials attached to the instance. Much safer than embedding AWS keys in code.

questions.question: What does 'p3.8xlarge' mean in AWS instance naming?
options: Instance family (P=GPU), generation 3, 8xlarge size (4 V100 GPUs)
questions.explanation: P=GPU family. 3=generation. 8xlarge=size (4x V100 GPUs, 32 vCPUs, 244GB RAM). Larger suffix = more GPUs and RAM.

questions.question: How do you transfer training data from S3 to an EC2 instance efficiently?
options: aws s3 sync s3://bucket/data/ /local/data/ or mount S3 with s3fs
questions.explanation: aws s3 sync is fast and resumable. For very large datasets, consider AWS DataSync or streaming directly from S3 in the training script.

questions.question: What is NVIDIA CUDA and why is it needed for GPU training?
options: A parallel computing platform that allows code to run on NVIDIA GPUs
questions.explanation: CUDA = Compute Unified Device Architecture. PyTorch/TF use CUDA to offload tensor operations to the GPU. Without CUDA drivers, GPU acceleration is unavailable.

questions.question: What is the recommended way to stop an EC2 instance after training to avoid charges?
options: Stop (not Terminate) the instance — preserves EBS volume but stops compute billing
Leave it running for next time
questions.explanation: Stop = pauses compute billing, preserves data on EBS. Terminate = deletes instance AND storage. Accidentally terminating loses all work.

questions.question: EC2 instance store vs EBS for ML workloads:
options: Instance store: faster, ephemeral (lost on stop/terminate). EBS: persistent, slower. Use EBS for checkpoints, instance store for temp scratch space.
questions.explanation: NVMe instance store SSDs are very fast for I/O but data is lost when instance stops. Always save model checkpoints to EBS or S3.

questions.question: What is Deep Learning AMI (Amazon Machine Image)?
options: A pre-configured OS image with CUDA, PyTorch, TF, and ML tools pre-installed
questions.explanation: AWS provides Deep Learning AMIs so you don't spend hours installing CUDA, cuDNN, PyTorch, etc. Start training in minutes instead of hours.
